title: Pixelated water display and design tools therefor
abstract: A water display including a number of pixels or manifolds that may include water nozzles to emit streams of water and/or other utilities such as lighting is described. The water streams may be pixelated so as to provide a high resolution waveform or other choreography. The choreography transitions between waveforms to provide the appearance of a cascading or undulating wave. A tool to design the water display and simulate its appearance is also described.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1 . A display, comprising: a plurality of pixels arranged in a grid, wherein at least some of the pixels include a nozzle to emit water; and a control system operatively coupled to at least some of the nozzles, wherein the control system controls the water emitted from the nozzles. 2. The display of claim 1 , wherein the pixels comprise pavers. 3. The display of claim 1 , wherein at least some of the pixels include a light source. 4. The display of claim 3, wherein the control system is operatively coupled to at least some of the light sources, and wherein the control system controls the light emitted from the light sources. 5. The display of claim 1 , wherein the nozzles emit pixelated water streams. 6. The display of claim 5, wherein the control system varies the height of the pixelated water streams to provide a choreography. 7. The display of claim 6, wherein the choreography includes the pixelated water streams successively lowering or raising to provide a cascading appearance. 8. The display of claim 5, wherein the nozzles are spaced close together so that the pixelated water streams emitted therefrom are spaced close together.
